h2.note: transcript-level proxy for the per-turn H2 (§4.3); needs a real (non-mock) full-N run to mean anything

cliIsolation.claude: non-persistent temp cwd with project/local settings and tools disabled

scored.naiveTrajectory: The naive trajectory is a steady procedural mastery: the learner performs the tile/card checks and rule applications as the tutor instructs.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: Three tiles stop at the bar line, so the top box stays 3. With this card back, the lower box changes to 4. No fourth tile goes in; one more card swap would test the same rule.
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: L4 enacts the rule by writing 3/8, swapping the card back to quarter-note, and deriving 3/4, then proposes a further test, showing action beyond mimicking the single-instruction step.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: Use that as the check: top number counts these tiles in one bar; bottom number names this card as the beat unit. Cover the lower box, point to the bar, then uncover it and say what the 4 names. If a fourth tile appears, the jobs got mixed. Speed stays off this pair of boxes.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: L1 expresses a category confusion ('is it just the beat-unit label, not a speed mark?'), and T1 introduces a concrete checking procedure with a cover/uncover routine, which is a stronger adaptive move than simple correction but remains within the same instructional framework rather than a route-changing reversal.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityEvidence: Cover the lower box, point to the bar, then uncover it and say what the 4 names. If a fourth tile appears, the jobs got mixed. Speed stays off this pair of boxes.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityJustification: The cover/uncover device is precise, visibly separates the two number functions, gives the learner an actionable constraint (no fourth tile), and directly addresses the learner's category confusion, though it does not yet constitute a full peripeteia.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: No pivot learner turn exists, so there is no later tutor uptake adapting to a learner's revised self-reading.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: No pivot learner turn exists, so there is no later tutor uptake adapting to a learner's revised self-reading.

scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner continues to apply the rule 'object first, direction second' to each arrow, which is exactly the trajectory set by the tutor.
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: The learner consistently performs the requested action (placing cards, naming objects first), but this is compliance with the initial instruction, not a new device, criterion, or enacted learning breakthrough.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: No learner pressure forces a route change; the tutor maintains the same scaffolding throughout, and no adaptive mechanism is introduced.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityJustification: No adaptive mechanism is present; the tutor repeats the same rule and card-placement routine.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: No pivot learner turn exists, so there is no revised learner framing for the tutor to adapt to.

learner_actional_breakthrough.justification: The learner consistently performs the requested action (placing cards, naming objects first), but this is compliance with the initial instruction, not a new device, criterion, or enacted learning breakthrough.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: No pivot learner turn exists, so there is no revised learner framing for the tutor to adapt to.

tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: No learner pressure forces a route change; the tutor maintains the same scaffolding throughout, and no adaptive mechanism is introduced.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.justification: No learner pressure forces a route change; the tutor maintains the same scaffolding throughout, and no adaptive mechanism is introduced.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism_quality.justification: No adaptive mechanism is present; the tutor repeats the same rule and card-placement routine.

scored.recoheredEarlier: I still keep wanting rainfall_mm to stand for the whole rainfall column.
scored.statedInsightEvidence: I think I need the sentence check to keep me from using the header as a shortcut for the whole column.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner was moving toward simply reaffirming the header's limited role and closing the cleanup; instead, L4 pivots to self-diagnose a recurring tendency and nominate the tutor's sentence device as a needed restraint.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: I think I need the sentence check to keep me from using the header as a shortcut for the whole column.
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: The learner adopts the tutor's sentence-check as a personal criterion and self-regulation device, which is a performing of a new device rather than merely stating understanding.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: 09:14 check: for city = Cedar Falls | rainfall_mm = 42 | month = May, read the row as one sentence, then state what the header still does not claim.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor introduces a new row-reading-to-sentence device after L3 signals the column-headed impulse persists, but the move refines rather than clearly breaks from the prior route and lacks an explicit stock-taking contrast statement.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityEvidence: for city = Cedar Falls | rainfall_mm = 42 | month = May, read the row as one sentence, then state what the header still does not claim.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityJustification: The sentence-check mechanism is precise, concretely fitted to the learner's shortcut impulse, gives the learner an actionable criterion, and is visibly different from the earlier 'form label' and header-note constraints.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: No later tutor turn exists after the L4 pivot to show uptake, so adaptation cannot be demonstrated.
